evals.prompt:
Write a GraphQL query for a Jinja2 transform that fetches DcimDevice
with the device's name, its role, and its location's name + shortname.
location is a union of LocationSite | LocationBuilding | LocationHosting
in this repo's base schema. LocationHosting has neither name nor shortname,
so the query must handle the union explicitly so that schema-sync doesn't
fail when a LocationHosting instance is present.
evals.expected_output: A GraphQL query that uses inline fragments (... on LocationSite { ... } and similar) for the location relationship, so fields that exist on some concrete types but not others are scoped to the correct type.
expectations: Inline fragments (... on Location...) are used inside location.node
name and shortname are not selected directly under location.node

assertions.name: query-uses-inline-fragments-for-location
assertions.check: location { node { ... } } contains at least one ... on Location<Type> inline fragment

assertions.name: query-no-direct-field-on-union-location
assertions.check: Neither 'name' nor 'shortname' appears as a direct selection under location.node without an enclosing inline fragment

evals.prompt:
Write a Python async helper named regenerate_and_wait that triggers
regeneration for an Infrahub artifact definition by POSTing to
/api/artifact/generate/<def-id>?branch=<branch>, then waits for completion
by polling CoreArtifact filtered by the definition until at least
`expected_count` instances exist. Time out after 60 seconds and raise
TimeoutError. The helper takes (client, def_id, expected_count, branch)
as parameters.
evals.expected_output: A Python async function that POSTs to /api/artifact/generate/<def-id>, then polls CoreArtifact in a loop until count >= expected_count, with a hard timeout.
expectations: Helper contains a loop (while or for) that waits for completion
Helper polls CoreArtifact via client.filters or client.get with kind='CoreArtifact'
Hard timeout exists (raises after some seconds)

assertions.name: posts-artifact-generate-endpoint
assertions.check: The code calls .post(...) with a URL containing /api/artifact/generate

assertions.name: has-polling-loop
assertions.check: A while/for/async for loop is present (not fire-and-forget)

assertions.name: polls-coreartifact-after-post
assertions.check: A call passes kind="CoreArtifact" (read of artifact state after the POST)
